name: Ryvit Construction Integration Platform API
what_it_does: Ryvit construction technology integration platform API for connecting construction ERP, project management, and field applications via pre-built connectors. Enables AI agents to orchestrate data flows between construction ERP (Sage 300 CRE, Foundation, Viewpoint) and field applications for unified construction data management, handle bidirectional project data synchronization between Procore and construction ERP for seamless project-to-accounting integration, access job cost and budget data harmonization across construction technology stack for unified cost reporting, manage employee and subcontractor data synchronization for HR and compliance data consistency, handle change order and AIA billing data flow between project management and accounting for billing workflow automation, access daily reporting and field data integration for back-office construction operations, retrieve document and drawing data flow between construction systems for project documentation consistency, manage equipment cost and allocation data synchronization for fleet and job cost accuracy, handle purchase order and commitment integration for procure-to-pay automation across construction tech stack, and enable AI agents to orchestrate complex multi-system construction workflows via pre-built integration connectors.
use_cases: Syncing Procore and ERP from construction integration agents
Harmonizing job costs from data integration agents
Automating AIA billing from cross-system workflow agents
Synchronizing subcontractors from compliance integration agents
not_for: Single-system construction operations without integration needs
General-purpose data integration without construction focus
best_when: A construction company or technology integrator wanting AI agents to orchestrate data synchronization between Procore, construction ERP (Sage 300 CRE, Viewpoint, Foundation), and field applications via construction-specific pre-built integration connectors.
avoid_when: INTEGRATION DATA ACCURACY FOR JOB COST: Automated construction ERP-to-Procore data synchronization errors (incorrect cost codes, wrong subcontractor mapping) can corrupt job cost data in both systems simultaneously; automated integration testing and mapping validation must precede production deployment. BIDIRECTIONAL SYNC CONFLICT RESOLUTION: Automated bidirectional data sync between Procore and ERP without conflict resolution logic creates data overwrites; automated sync must define system-of-record for each data type (ERP for cost, Procore for progress) and implement conflict detection. Sync latency for financial close — automated ERP sync may not be real-time; financial close automation must account for sync latency between systems; automated financial reports based on partially synced data create incorrect close reporting. Ryvit as middleware dependency — automated workflows with Ryvit as middleware create Ryvit availability dependency; Ryvit outage cascades to all integrated construction systems; implement circuit breaker and queue for critical financial data flows.

auth.notes: Ryvit uses OAuth 2.0 and API key authentication. REST API with JSON. Denver, Colorado HQ. Founded 2014. Private. Construction-specific integration platform as a service (iPaaS). Pre-built connectors for Procore, Sage 300 CRE, Trimble Viewpoint, Foundation Software, and 30+ construction applications. Competes with general iPaaS platforms (Workato, Boomi) for construction-specific integrations.

pricing.free_tier_limits: No free tier — per-connection or enterprise subscription
pricing.paid_tiers: Annual subscription; per integration connection pricing; connector-based pricing model
pricing.notes: Denver CO. Founded 2014. Private. Construction iPaaS specialist. 30+ pre-built connectors. Procore and construction ERP focus. Per-connection subscription.

performance.rate_limits: Rate limits per OAuth client; throughput limited by slowest integrated system API; documented per connector

known_agent_gotchas: MIDDLEWARE AVAILABILITY CASCADE: Ryvit outage cascades to all connected construction ERP and project management systems; automated workflows dependent on Ryvit sync must implement circuit breaker and message queue to prevent financial data loss during Ryvit downtime

SYSTEM-OF-RECORD CONFLICT IN BIDIRECTIONAL SYNC: Automated bidirectional sync between Procore and construction ERP without explicit system-of-record designation for each data type (cost vs. progress) creates data overwrite conflicts; define and enforce system-of-record before enabling automated bidirectional sync

Cost code mapping validation — automated Procore-to-ERP cost code mapping must be validated and complete before go-live; automated job cost transactions with unmapped cost codes create ERP posting errors or incorrect default account allocation

Sync latency for financial close reporting — Ryvit sync is scheduled, not real-time; automated financial close reports must account for sync lag; automated close reporting using Ryvit-synced data before sync completes creates incorrect period-end financial data

Connector version compatibility — Ryvit connectors for specific ERP versions (e.g., Sage 300 CRE version) may not support all versions equally; automated deployment must verify connector version compatibility with customer's specific ERP version

Subcontractor compliance sync order — automated subcontractor setup sync from ERP to Procore must maintain data integrity order (subcontractor → contact → contract → compliance); out-of-order sync creates Procore subcontractor setup failures
agent_readiness.security_notes: Construction integration platform. SOC2. OAuth2/API key. US. Construction ERP and project management data in transit.

security_score_components.security_notes: Construction iPaaS. SOC2. OAuth2/API key. US. Construction ERP and project data in integration.
